challenges.title: Contact Form
challenges.description: Create a contact form with an action attribute and method="post". Include text inputs for name and email, a textarea for a message, and a submit button. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a contact form with action, method, inputs, textarea, and submit -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert 'action=' in html.lower(), 'Form should have an action attribute'
assert 'method="post"' in html.lower(), 'Form should have method="post"'
assert html.lower().count('<input') >= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 input elements'
assert '<textarea' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <textarea> element'
assert '</textarea>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <textarea> element'
assert 'type="submit"' in html.lower() or '<button' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a submit button'
hints: Add <textarea name="message"></textarea> for the message field

challenges.title: Fieldset Form
challenges.description: Create a form that uses fieldset and legend elements to group related inputs. Include at least two fieldset groups, each with a legend and at least two labeled inputs. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a form with fieldset and legend grouping -->
<!-- Include at least two groups of inputs -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert html.lower().count('<fieldset') >= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 fieldset elements'
assert html.lower().count('</fieldset>') >= 2, 'Should close all fieldset elements'
assert html.lower().count('<legend') >= 2, 'Each fieldset should have a legend element'
assert html.lower().count('</legend>') >= 2, 'Should close all legend elements'
assert html.lower().count('<input') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 input elements'
assert html.lower().count('<label') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 label elements'
hints: Wrap related inputs in <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
For example, group personal info inputs and address inputs separately
Each input should still have its own label
